title: Circuit-Specific Warmup And Undercut Regime Priors
summary: Official F1 strategy pieces note that low-degradation, low-energy tracks can favor overcuts because fresh tyres warm slowly, while higher-degradation tracks strengthen the undercut.
rationale: Official F1 strategy pieces note that low-degradation, low-energy tracks can favor overcuts because fresh tyres warm slowly, while higher-degradation tracks strengthen the undercut. That suggests a circuit-level regime prior, not a single global tyre-life mapping.
implementation_hint: From training folds only, estimate per Race or Race_Year priors such as median stint length, overcut-favorability proxy, and compound-specific stop-lap hazard curves. Use these as base priors or residual targets, then let the main model learn deviations from circuit norms. A separate expert for slick low-energy circuits vs wet/high-deg circuits is also testable.
expected_effect: Should reduce bias from one-size-fits-all expected tyre life and improve transfer across circuits with very different warmup/degradation behavior.
risk: With only 26 races, circuit priors can overfit fast; they need shrinkage, fold-wise generation, and possibly fallback to broader compound/weather buckets.
